title: multi-stage builds fail to resolve named image if build arg env is in name
body:
**Description**

Multi-stage builds fail to resolve a named image if a build arg  is in the name.  A reproducer is below.

```
ARG my_env=a

FROM scratch as stage_a
RUN /bin/true

FROM stage-${my_env} as stage_b
RUN /bin/true
```

I was trying to build https://github.com/zalando/spilo/blob/master/postgres-appliance/Dockerfile which uses a build arg in `FROM` at a later stage.

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**
```
buildah-1.27.0-1.fc37.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.27.0
Go Version:      go1.19
Image Spec:      1.0.2-dev
Runtime Spec:    1.0.2-dev
CNI Spec:        1.0.0
libcni Version:  v1.1.2
image Version:   5.22.0
Git Commit:
Built:           Tue Aug  9 04:07:39 2022
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
BuildPlatform:   linux/amd64
```

comments.body:
@ibotty Following works if `--build-arg` is explicitly specified.

Try
```Dockerfile
ARG my_env=a

FROM alpine as stage_a
RUN /bin/true

FROM stage_${my_env} as stage_b
RUN /bin/true
```

Command
```sh
buildah build -t test --build-arg my_env=a .
```

Output
```console
buildah build -t test --build-arg my_env=a .
[1/2] STEP 1/2: FROM alpine AS stage_a
[1/2] STEP 2/2: RUN /bin/true
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 994393dc58e7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a5b2c723feaa done  
Copying config 411f196471 done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 411f196471a
[2/2] STEP 1/2: FROM 411f196471a4b7a2f8ac1603c5b5e681357b59eaa23912ebea9df450215a22f5 AS stage_b
[2/2] STEP 2/2: RUN /bin/true
[2/2] COMMIT test
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob 994393dc58e7 skipped: already exists  
Copying blob a5b2c723feaa skipped: already exists  
Copying blob 810d1a8a7a6d done  
Copying config 078f1ab8cd done  
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> 078f1ab8cda
Successfully tagged localhost/test:latest
078f1ab8cda8c763d48359ec8b832c07a3d9bd6de609383d5f3dd9434f235436
```

Issue is only that default value of `build_arg` is ignored which needs to be fixed.
Thanks for reporting PR https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4366 should close this.
